body_txt: You need someone to help maintain a location, whether it be for yourself or someone else. Unforunately you just can't get the staff these days... so where else better to pull from than the dead from those days? This can take many forms, be it a ghostly maid or a zombie butler . Their status as undead may seem like a boon: no longer needing rest, food and drink allows them to be an Eternal Employee . However, certain kinds may come with more drawbacks than others. A phantom may be able to go through walls and thus perform tasks more quickly, but a shambling ghoul is more likely to break something, probably valuable, by accident. As a result, the zombie kind tends to be more of the Bumbling Sidekick variety. There are many ways someone might come into possession of one: perhaps they just happened to come with the Haunted House you inherited, maybe they've been serving your family for generations , even from beyond the grave, or maybe they were The Jeeves for you in life, died too soon and were just too good to replace (nothing a little necromancy can't fix). In some instances, their masters may also be deceased and may too have entered undeath, with their faithful servants aiding them in death, as they did in life. Alternatively, the master may have long passed, but their staff remain at their posts long after to be encountered by some poor unfortunate soul. As undead, some can serve as a very strong Battle Butler . See also Afterlife of Service and Absurdly Dedicated Worker . Compare Undead Laborers , which is for a general workforce and often manual labor or unskilled jobs, Robot Maid , for when the servant is similarly needless and immortal but made of metal and circuits rather than ectoplasm or bones, and Creepy Housekeeper . Not to be confused with Ghost Butler , which is when doors close behind people with no explanation, or Night of the Living Mooks when they are undead Mooks for battle rather than staff for service and maintenance. Contrast The Renfield , who is a human servant of a vampire, and The Necrocracy , for societies where the undead rule the living. Compare and contrast The Igor , who is not strictly undead but is definitely abnormal.
